home.description: I'm Simon Sohneg, a certified developer and former baker. Driven by curiosity and a love for creating things, I turned my passion into a career. Now I build websites, apps, software and games.

about.title: About me
about.subtitle: A journey from flour to code
about.chapter1Text: For 10 years, the bakery was my home. First in a small shop where I learned the craft by hand, with the best sourdough around. Then in larger operations. Every morning at 4 AM, turning simple ingredients into something delicious. I loved making people happy.
about.chapter2Text: Over time I developed a flour allergy. It kept getting worse, until one day I couldn't breathe anymore. I kept going because I loved the job. But eventually I had to let go.
about.chapter3Text: Disability insurance enabled me to start a second apprenticeship and Schindler Elevators gave me the chance. Over four years I learned everything from the ground up as a fullstack developer: Golang, C#, Angular, ASP.NET Core, Unity, Docker and more. Thanks to strong performance, I was accepted into the internal talent program. I built a fallback website for elevator emergency communication, developed a VR application for showcasing lift models, an AR app for work instructions, and a check-in app for school visit days. Later, I even got to work in R&D on the top-secret Schindler X8. As a kid I always said: baker or programmer. Now I'm both.
about.chapter4Text: Since September 2025 I've been at Brabender Solutions. Together with the CEO, I'm overhauling the internal systems. I'm rebuilding the complete product management, CRM and ERP from scratch. From employee management to resource planning, all in one system. My toolkit: React, XAF, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, Docker and Nginx Proxy Manager. For development I rely heavily on AI: Claude Code as a development partner, MCP servers for connecting external tools and data sources, custom skills for recurring workflows and AI agents that handle tasks autonomously.
about.chapter4TextShort: Since September 2025 I've been at Brabender Solutions. Together with the CEO, I'm rebuilding the complete product management, CRM and ERP from scratch. Built with React, XAF and ASP.NET Core, powered by AI with Claude Code, MCP servers and custom skills.
about.chapter5Text: Tuning Schweiz started in 2016 from a simple idea: anyone looking for a tuning event in Switzerland had to dig through countless Facebook groups, websites and forums. There was no central place. It all began with organizing my own event. That's when I realized: the scene is huge, but completely scattered. So Tuning Schweiz became the platform that brings all events, news and the community together in one place. Today over 9,000 members. And in 2026, the next step followed: with the SimRacing team Alpineers, it goes from the street to the virtual track.
about.chapter6Text: My current passion project: DICE OF FATE, a fantasy roguelite where a mortal challenges the gods of the old world at their own game. The mechanics combine a familiar game concept (Yahtzee) with a modern roguelite meta-layer and joker-like special dice. In the world of Aethoria, the gods don't rule through wars or prayers, but through the dice game of fate. What the gods roll, happens: a bad roll from the earth god means earthquakes, a good roll from the sea god means rich harvests. Your goal? Steal the gods' power, roll by roll.
about.chapter7Text: Beyond development, I also run the infrastructure behind it all. Windows Server for the SimRacing ACC server, Linux servers at Hetzner for client websites. My workflow: Docker, Harbor, Portainer and Nginx Proxy Manager for clean deployments. From code to hosting, everything from one hand.
about.chapter8Text: When I'm not coding, I make electronic music as Sonex or DJ in VRChat. And our two Pomskies Ari and Luci make sure I get enough fresh air.
about.closingQuote: I still make cookies, they just have a different meaning now.

tuningSchweiz.title: Tuning Schweiz App
tuningSchweiz.description: A custom event app for Switzerland's largest car community with over 8,500 members. Features event management, meetup coordination, and community features.
tuningSchweiz.thought: My passion project! Started as a small idea and grew into something amazing.

businessSystem.title: Business Management System
businessSystem.description: A complete enterprise solution including CRM, time tracking, document management, and workflow automation. Built for Brabender Solutions.
businessSystem.thought: Learning so much about enterprise architecture here!

vrElevator.title: VR Marketing Experience
vrElevator.description: An immersive virtual reality application for elevator manufacturers, allowing customers to experience products in a virtual showroom.
vrElevator.thought: My first VR project – seeing people's reactions was priceless!

dogCamera.title: Pomsky Cam
dogCamera.description: A Raspberry Pi-powered dog camera that monitors our two Pomskies and sends real-time updates to Discord when it detects activity.
dogCamera.thought: Built this because I missed our fluffballs during work!

crowDesk.title: Crow Desk
crowDesk.description: A professional task management platform for teams, featuring project organization, task tracking, team collaboration, and a client portal with real-time updates.
crowDesk.thought: A challenging project that pushed my full-stack skills!

syncShift.title: Sync Shift
syncShift.description: A mobile time tracking app developed for an industrial company. Employees can start, pause, and end shifts, as well as add notes – available on Android & iOS.
syncShift.thought: Simple concept, but the details made it interesting!

clientWebsites.title: Client Websites
clientWebsites.description: Various websites built for local businesses and organizations using WordPress, featuring custom designs and functionality tailored to each client's needs.
clientWebsites.thought: Love helping local businesses get online!

metadata.title: My Portfolio
metadata.description: Certified developer for websites, apps, software and games
